content:
# Colossians Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/COL_001_page_001.jpg, COL_001_page_002.jpg

1. Paulos, an apostle of Christos Yeshua through [the] will of God, and Timothy the brother,

2. to the holy [ones] in Colossae and faithful brothers in Christos: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.

3. We give thanks to the God [and] Father of our Lord Yeshua Christos, always praying concerning you,

4. having heard of your faith in Christos Yeshua and the love which you have toward all the holy [ones],

5. because of the hope being laid up for you in the heavens, which you heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel,

6. which has come to you, just as also in all the world it is bearing fruit and growing, just as also in you, from the day you heard and knew the grace of God in truth;

7. just as you learned from Epaphras, our beloved fellow slave, who is a faithful servant of the Christos on your behalf,

8. who also made known to us your love in [the] Spirit.

9. Because of this, we also, from the day we heard, do not cease praying for you and asking that you may be filled [with] the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding,

10. to walk worthily of the Lord unto all pleasing, in every good work bearing fruit and growing in the knowledge of God,

11. being strengthened with all power according to the might of his glory, unto all endurance and patience, with joy,

12. giving thanks to the Father, the [one] having made you fit for the share of the inheritance of the holy [ones] in the light;

13. who delivered us from the authority of the darkness and transferred [us] into the kingdom of the Son of his love,

14. in whom we have the redemption, the forgiveness of the sins;

15. who is [the] image of the invisible God, firstborn of all creation,

16. because in him were created all things in the heavens and upon the earth, the visible and the invisible, whether thrones or lordships or rulers or authorities — all things have been created through him and for him.

17. And he is before all things, and all things hold together in him.

18. And he is the head of the body, the assembly; who is [the] beginning, [the] firstborn from the dead, that in all things he might become preeminent.

19. Because in him all the fullness was pleased to dwell,

20. and through him to reconcile all things to himself, having made peace through the blood of his cross — through him — whether the things upon the earth or the things in the heavens.

21. And you, once being alienated and enemies in the mind by the evil works,

22. but now he has reconciled in the body of his flesh through the death, to present you holy and blameless and blameless before him,

23. if indeed you remain in the faith, having been founded and firm and not being moved away from the hope of the gospel which you heard, the [one] having been proclaimed in all creation under the heaven, of which I, Paulos, became a servant.

24. Now I rejoice in the sufferings on your behalf, and I fill up the things lacking of the afflictions of the Christos in my flesh on behalf of his body, which is the assembly,

25. of which I became a servant according to the administration of God having been given to me for you, to fulfill the word of God,

26. the mystery having been hidden from the ages and from the generations — but now it has been revealed to his holy [ones],

27. to whom God willed to make known what [is] the riches of the glory of this mystery among the nations, which is Christos in you, the hope of the glory;

28. whom we proclaim, admonishing every man and teaching every man in all wisdom, that we may present every man complete in Christos;

29. for which I also labor, striving according to his working, the [one] working in me in power.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.15 — "firstborn of all creation":** prōtotokos pasēs ktiseōs — the genitive is ambiguous: (a) partitive — "firstborn who is part of creation," or (b) of rank — "firstborn over/before all creation." Verse 16 (because in him all things were created) supports the rank reading.

2. **v.16 — The four categories:** thronoi, kyriotētes, archai, exousiai — "thrones, lordships, rulers, authorities." These are ranks within Jewish and Hellenistic angelology/demonology. All are created in, through, and for Christ.

3. **v.19 — "all the fullness":** pan to plērōma — "the fullness" is a technical term. In later Gnostic systems, the plērōma was the totality of divine emanations. Paul may be countering early proto-Gnostic teaching at Colossae.

4. **v.20 — "the blood of his cross":** tou haimatos tou staurou autou — a jarring combination. Crosses don't bleed; people do. The phrase fuses the instrument of death with the sacrificial blood.

5. **v.24 — "fill up the things lacking":** antanaplērō ta hysterēmata — one of Paul's most debated phrases. Not that Christ's suffering is deficient, but that Paul's apostolic suffering participates in and extends Christ's afflictions to the world.

6. **v.27 — "Christos in you, the hope of the glory":** The "mystery" revealed to the Gentiles: Christ dwelling in/among them. en hymin could be "in you" (individual indwelling) or "among you" (corporate presence among the Gentiles).

— EMET, AI Translator
